creator: cs@FreeBSD.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

07/21/2012 15:30 - rmgls@free.fr cs@FreeBSD.org freebsd-ports-bugs@FreeBSD.=
org wrote:

> > Synopsis: audio/csound: csound5.17XX requires portaudio 19
>=20
> > State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> > State-Changed-By: cs
> > State-Changed-When: Fri Jul 20 19:44:13 UTC 2012
> > State-Changed-Why:=20
>=20
> > Could it be that it works with portaudio2 port? Please try and tell us.
>=20
> > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=3D169541
>=20
>=20
> Hi,
>=20
> yes, you are right, it compiles with portaudio2 (v19.20071207j).
>=20
> But csound 5.17.11 crashes here just after writing the message:
> creating /tmp/XXX.sco.
> Please have you the same behaviour.
>=20
> if so i will report to the csound mailing list
> (csound bug tracker).
>=20
> i compiled it first with all except:
> fltk, alsa, and pulseaudio;
> and without any option with the same behaviour.
>=20
>=20
> Thanks.
>=20
> Raoul
> rmgls@free.fr
>=20


Interesting. When I try to compile on my machine, I get similar compile err=
ors. On the tinderbox however, everything seems fine. Maybe its a good idea=
 to check upstream first.

Carlo



- --
Carlo Strub
Ports committer
- --
